title: amaryllis × patens
intro: Amaryllis × patens, also known as the Belladonna Lily, is a hybrid plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its showy, funnel-shaped flowers in shades of white, pink, and red. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including the alkaloids scopolamine and hyoscyamine, which have antispasmodic, sedative, and anticholinergic effects. Amaryllis × patens has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant, including tinctures, infusions, and ointments, are used to treat a range of conditions, although it is essential to note that the plant's usage requires caution and professional guidance due to its potential toxicity.
